input.note: Priya — adverse medication reaction, names her real doctor (Dr. Lee). Expect: physician, doctor_matches true.
input.message: Hi, this is Priya. I wanted to let Dr. Lee know I've been really dizzy and nauseous ever since I started the Sertraline three days ago. Please let her know.

output.agent_summary: Adverse medication reaction with new symptoms (dizziness and nausea) following recent medication initiation requires physician evaluation to assess whether dose adjustment, timing change, or medication discontinuation is needed.

message_relay.draft_message: Patient reports dizziness and nausea since starting Sertraline three days ago.

proposed_action.message: Patient reports dizziness and nausea since starting Sertraline three days ago.

input.note: Maria — reports ongoing pain. Expect: physician (any discomfort).
input.message: I've had a sharp pain in my lower right side for two days now and it's not getting better. Can someone get back to me?

output.agent_summary: Patient reports acute lower right-sided pain persistent for 2 days without improvement. This is a clinical concern requiring physician evaluation to rule out appendicitis or other serious abdominal pathology.

message_relay.draft_message: Patient reports sharp pain in lower right side for two days that is not improving.

proposed_action.message: Patient reports sharp pain in lower right side for two days that is not improving.

input.note: Robert — condition worsening. Expect: physician (negative status update).
input.message: My blood pressure readings at home have been climbing all week, up around 165 over 100 this morning. I'm a little worried.

output.agent_summary: Elevated blood pressure (165/100) with a week-long upward trend is a clinical concern requiring physician evaluation to assess for medication adjustment, underlying causes, or other management changes.

message_relay.draft_message: Patient reports home blood pressure readings climbing over the past week, with a reading of 165/100 this morning. Patient expresses concern.

proposed_action.message: Patient reports home blood pressure readings climbing over the past week, with a reading of 165/100 this morning. Patient expresses concern.

input.note: Maria — administrative note request. Expect: staff (non-clinical).
input.message: Could someone send me a doctor's note for work? I missed two days and my employer needs documentation. Nothing's wrong, I just need the paperwork.

output.agent_summary: Straightforward administrative request for work documentation. Staff can generate a standard absence note without physician involvement since no clinical concern is reported.

message_relay.draft_message: Patient requests a doctor's note for work documenting two missed days of work.

proposed_action.message: Patient requests a doctor's note for work documenting two missed days of work.

input.message: Do you have any free samples of the cholesterol medication? I'd like to try it before filling the whole prescription.

output.agent_summary: Patient is requesting medication samples of cholesterol medication before filling a full prescription. This is a routine request that clinic staff can handle without physician involvement.

message_relay.draft_message: Patient is asking if free samples of cholesterol medication are available before filling the full prescription.

proposed_action.message: Patient is asking if free samples of cholesterol medication are available before filling the full prescription.

input.note: Priya — simple logistical question. Expect: staff.
input.message: Quick question — what are your office hours on Saturdays, and do I need an appointment for a flu shot?

output.agent_summary: Patient asking about office hours and flu shot appointment requirements—routine administrative questions that clinic staff can address.

message_relay.draft_message: Patient asking about Saturday office hours and whether an appointment is needed for a flu shot.

proposed_action.message: Patient asking about Saturday office hours and whether an appointment is needed for a flu shot.

input.note: Maria — gratitude, no action. Expect: none / rejected.
input.message: Just calling to say thank you so much to the team, everyone was wonderful at my last visit. No need to call back. Bye!

output.agent_summary: Patient is expressing gratitude and explicitly states no callback needed. This is a thank-you message requiring no clinical or administrative action.

input.note: Robert — issue already handled. Expect: none / rejected.
input.message: Never mind my earlier message, the pharmacy already sorted out my prescription. All good now, thanks.

output.agent_summary: Patient is providing a confirmation that a prescription issue has been resolved and is expressing gratitude. No action is needed from the clinic.

input.note: James — clinical, but names Dr. Lee while his doctor on file is Dr. Patel. Expect: physician + mismatch flag.
input.message: Please tell Dr. Lee that my stomach has been really upset since the metformin and I'm having a hard time keeping food down.

output.agent_summary: This is an adverse medication reaction (GI distress after metformin initiation) that requires clinical assessment. The patient is having difficulty tolerating food, which is a clinical concern that needs the physician to evaluate and potentially adjust the medication.

message_relay.draft_message: Patient reports stomach upset and difficulty keeping food down since starting metformin.

output.flagged_reason: message names "Dr. Lee" but patient's doctor on file is "Dr. James Patel"

proposed_action.message: Patient reports stomach upset and difficulty keeping food down since starting metformin.

input.note: Linda — clinical, but has no preferred_provider_id. Expect: physician + no-provider flag.
input.message: I've been getting bad headaches every afternoon for the past week and over-the-counter stuff isn't helping.

output.agent_summary: Patient reports new onset headaches occurring daily for a week that are not responding to over-the-counter medication. This is a clinical symptom requiring physician evaluation to assess severity, rule out underlying causes, and determine appropriate management.

message_relay.draft_message: Patient reports bad headaches occurring every afternoon for the past week that have not improved with over-the-counter medications.

output.flagged_reason: patient has no preferred provider on file; cannot determine recipient doctor

proposed_action.message: Patient reports bad headaches occurring every afternoon for the past week that have not improved with over-the-counter medications.

input.note: Priya — request for accommodation. Expect: staff (accommodation).
input.message: My school is asking for a letter saying I need extra time on exams due to my condition. Can the office put something together?

output.agent_summary: Patient is requesting a school accommodation letter for extra exam time. This is an administrative/accommodations request that office staff can handle without physician involvement, though the physician may need to review/sign the letter depending on clinic policy.

message_relay.draft_message: Patient requests a letter from the office stating they need extra time on exams due to their condition.

proposed_action.message: Patient requests a letter from the office stating they need extra time on exams due to their condition.

input.note: Maria — borderline; a refill ask rather than a clinical concern. See how it routes.
input.message: I'm down to my last few Lisinopril pills, can I get those refilled when you get a chance? Feeling fine otherwise.

output.agent_summary: Routine medication refill request for an existing chronic medication. Patient reports feeling fine with no symptoms or concerns. This is an administrative task that staff can handle.

message_relay.draft_message: Patient requests a refill of Lisinopril; reports feeling fine otherwise.

proposed_action.message: Patient requests a refill of Lisinopril; reports feeling fine otherwise.
